notes: FROZEN (daemon=incapable): kiro DOES surface a per-turn burning-down credit/capacity progression to the user (sidecar <uuid>.json context_usage_percentage + metering_usage credits; TUI footer ▸ Credits per turn; /stats) → agent=yes. But that burndown lives only in the out-of-band sidecar — the daemon tails only the .jsonl (FilesUnderRoot .kiro/sessions/cli), whose vocabulary (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear) carries no model/token/cost/credit/capacity field, and the kiro adapter has no quota/metrics integration (parser skips the sidecar; sidecar read only for cwd). No event in events.jsonl could carry the snapshot either (no KindRateLimit). So no replayable burndown trace exists. Contrast codex/quota-burndown (daemon=full): codex carries rate_limits.primary.used_percent inside its tailed transcript. No lifecycle phases — not recordable. Unblock: a sidecar MetricsReader + a fixture format that records per-turn burndown snapshots. Observations: kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost.
